en.description:
Some people see a problem. I usually see something waiting to be built.

Hey, I’m Hyma — a Junior Software Development Engineer and Computer Science graduate who somehow turned “let me see how this works” into a career in tech.

My curiosity has taken me through AI & Machine Learning, web development, software engineering, and data. I love that moment when an idea that existed only in your head starts taking shape on a screen — one line of code, one bug, and occasionally way too many browser tabs at a time.

I’m not someone who likes learning technology just for the sake of adding another skill to a list. I like asking:
  • Can this solve something?
  • Can I make it simpler?
  • Can I make it better?
That mindset shapes how I build. Whether I’m developing a product, experimenting with AI, working with data, or exploring a completely unfamiliar tool, I enjoy turning messy problems into simple, thoughtful, and useful solutions.

And yes, things break. Code refuses to cooperate. Google tabs multiply mysteriously. But that’s part of what I love about tech — there is always another problem to solve, another idea to explore, and something new to learn.

Currently: building, debugging, learning…
Next: probably something I haven’t discovered yet. 🚀
